2604.10989.title: MAFIG: Multi-agent Driven Formal Instruction Generation Framework
2604.10989.paper_summary: Emergency situations in scheduling systems often trigger local functional failures that undermine system stability and even cause system collapse. Existing methods primarily rely on robust scheduling or reactive scheduling, handling emergencies through predefined rules or rescheduling strategies. However, the diversity and unpredictability of real-world emergencies make them difficult to anticipate, which limits the adaptability of these methods in complex scenarios. Recent studies have shown that Large Language Models (LLMs) possess strong potential for complex scheduling tasks because of their extensive prior knowledge and strong reasoning capabilities. Nevertheless, the high inference latency of LLMs and the lengthy contextual information of scheduling systems significantly hinder their application for emergency handling. To mitigate these issues, we propose the Multi-agent Driven Formal Instruction Generation Framework (MAFIG). The framework constrains the decision scope to local functional modules affected by emergency situations and repairs scheduling logic rapidly by generating formal instructions. MAFIG contains a Perception Agent and an Emergency Decision Agent, which mitigates the adverse impact of lengthy system contexts on emergency decision-making. We further introduce span-focused loss-driven local distillation mechanism (SFL) to transfer the decision-making capability of powerful Cloud Large Language Models (C-LLMs) to lightweight local models, reducing inference latency while preserving decision-making effectiveness. Experiments in the Port, Warehousing, and Deck scheduling datasets show success rates of 98.49\%, 94.97\%, and 97.50\%, with average processing times of 0.33 s, 0.23 s, and 0.19 s. These results demonstrate that MAFIG effectively mitigates the impact of emergencies and improves the robustness and adaptability of scheduling systems.
2604.10989.paper_summary_zh: 緊急情況在排程系統中常常引發當地功能故障，這會破壞系統穩定性，甚至導致系統崩潰。現有的方法主要依賴穩健排程或反應式排程，通過預定義的規則或重新排程策略來處理緊急情況。然而，現實世界緊急情況的多樣性和不可預測性使其難以預測，這限制了這些方法在複雜場景中的適應性。最近的研究顯示，大型語言模型（LLMs）在複雜排程任務中具有強大的潛力，因為它們擁有廣泛的先驗知識和強大的推理能力。然而，LLMs 的高推理延遲和排程系統的冗長上下文信息顯著阻礙了它們在緊急處理中的應用。為了減輕這些問題，我們提出了多代理驅動的正式指令生成框架（MAFIG）。該框架將決策範圍限制在受緊急情況影響的當地功能模塊，並通過生成正式指令迅速修復排程邏輯。MAFIG 包含一個感知代理和一個緊急決策代理，這減輕了冗長系統上下文對緊急決策的負面影響。我們進一步引入了以跨度為中心的損失驅動本地蒸餾機制（SFL），以將強大的雲端大型語言模型（C-LLMs）的決策能力轉移到輕量級本地模型，從而在保持決策有效性的同時減少推理延遲。在港口、倉儲和甲板排程數據集中的實驗顯示成功率分別為 98.49\%、94.97\% 和 97.50\%，平均處理時間分別為 0.33 秒、0.23 秒和 0.19 秒。這些結果表明，MAFIG 有效減輕了緊急情況的影響，並改善了排程系統的穩健性和適應性。
2604.10989.author: Shixing Zhao et.al.
2604.10989.authors: Shixing Zhao, Zheng Si, Pengpeng Ouyang, Zhengqing Hu, Wanqi Zhu, Dong Chen, Yibo Guo, Mingliang Xu
